title: Implement the recursive _TDummyProbingMap for schema discovery
blocks: Dummy api skeleton — the skeleton's init pipeline calls `_TDummyProbingMap.probe<T>(fromJson)` to build its schema.

status_instructions.on_followup: If you found an edge case in `fromJson` styles (e.g. `json['x'] ?? fallback`), append it to the dummy api skeleton task's `side_context`.

description: Implement `lib/apis/_t_dummy_probing_map.dart` — a library-private `Map<String, dynamic>` subtype that is fed into a caller's `fromJson`, records every `[]` lookup, returns a type-correct stand-in, and widens failing casts by replaying `fromJson` until it completes without throwing. The final output is a recursive `TDummySchema` describing every field the DTO reads, with unresolved fields flagged. Nested DTOs are supported by returning a child `_TDummyProbingMap` whenever a caller casts a field to `Map<String, dynamic>`.
context_for_junior: json_serializable-style `fromJson` code reads fields with `json['field'] as SomeType`. We exploit that: we hand fromJson a `Map` that returns a default value for the type we are currently guessing, watch which key it read, and if the cast throws we step up to the next candidate type and replay fromJson. Each key settles on a resolved Type after at most N replays (N = candidate list size). For nested DTOs, the caller writes `NestedDto.fromJson(json['nested'] as Map<String, dynamic>)` — when the probing map sees that cast succeed, it returns a fresh child `_TDummyProbingMap`, which runs its own inner probing loop inside the parent's loop. The file is declared `part of 't_dummy_firestore_api.dart'` so only the dummy api can see it, and its name starts with `_` so the class itself is library-private.
why_it_matters: The probing map is how we avoid asking callers to hand-write schemas or annotate DTOs. Get it right once and every entity type in every feature auto-populates with zero ceremony. Get it wrong and the dummy api either misses fields, crashes on nested DTOs, or produces non-deterministic schema orders.
instructions: Create `lib/apis/_t_dummy_probing_map.dart` and declare it `part of 't_dummy_firestore_api.dart';` (the main file will be created in the next task — leave a comment TODO referencing that task if the part directive causes an analyzer complaint, and switch to a plain private class in the same file if the part-of wiring blocks analysis).
Define `class _TDummyProbingMap implements Map<String, dynamic>` with the state listed in the spec: `_resolved`, `_cursors`, `_unresolved`, `_lastKey`.
Implement `operator [](Object? key)` to: record the key as `_lastKey`, advance the cursor if needed, then return a type-correct stand-in for the current candidate type.
Implement the candidate order exactly as the spec lists: `[String, int, double, num, bool, DateTime, Timestamp, List<dynamic>, Map<String, dynamic>, GeoPoint, DocumentReference, Blob, null]`.
When the current candidate is `Map<String, dynamic>`, return a NEW `_TDummyProbingMap` instance — not a plain empty map. This is how nested DTOs auto-probe.
Implement `containsKey` → always true, `keys` → resolved keys, `length` → resolved count. Every remaining Map member delegates to the sensible empty default (e.g. `values` → empty iterable).

Implement `static TDummySchema probe<T>(T Function(Map<String, dynamic>) fromJson)` to run the widen-and-replay loop. On each iteration, wrap the `fromJson(probing)` call in try/catch; on catch, call `widenLastAccessedKey()` and retry. Bound attempts by `candidateCount * maxFields` (maxFields = 64 is a safe upper bound for a DTO).
Implement `widenLastAccessedKey()` to advance the last accessed key's cursor one step forward; when the candidate list is exhausted, add the key to `_unresolved`.

Implement `buildSchema()` to snapshot `_resolved` into `TDummySchemaLeaf` entries, recursively call `buildSchema()` on any child probing maps (those become `TDummySchemaBranch` entries), and produce a `TDummySchema` with the final `unresolved` set.

When the cast target for a field is a known converter type (e.g. `Timestamp` via `TimestampConverter`), the natural `json['field'] as Timestamp` path exercises the candidate list correctly — no special case needed.
Handle typed lists conservatively: when `List<dynamic>` is reached, return an empty `<dynamic>[]` and do not attempt to infer element types.
Run `make analyze package=turbo_firestore_api` and the new unit tests.
goals: One `probe<T>(fromJson)` call produces a complete, recursive `TDummySchema` for any json_serializable-style DTO in the monorepo.
Nested DTOs are auto-discovered through recursive child probing maps.
Unresolved fields are flagged without crashing the probe loop.
Termination is guaranteed by the bounded attempt counter.
non_goals: No element-type inference for typed lists.
No integration with the registry — that wiring happens in the next task.
acceptance_criteria: `make test package=turbo_firestore_api` passes new unit tests that probe a synthetic fixture DTO with flat fields.
A unit test with a synthetic nested DTO fixture produces a schema containing a `TDummySchemaBranch` for the nested field and the nested branch contains leaves for the nested DTO's fields.
A unit test with a synthetic DTO that reads a field the candidate list cannot satisfy (e.g. a custom converter expecting a domain type) results in that field landing in `unresolved`.
A unit test with a synthetic DTO reading `tags` as `List<String>` produces a `List<dynamic>` leaf entry and does NOT crash the probe loop.
constraints: Library-private visibility — the class must not leak out of the dummy api file's scope.
No workarounds if the part-of directive is blocked — switch to a plain private class and update the next task's `side_context` with the decision.
tools: RepoPrompt MCP for inspecting how monorepo DTOs write fromJson (e.g. lib/core/auth/dtos/user_dto.dart)
Write + Edit for the new file

testing.rules: No mocks. Write synthetic DTOs inside the test file itself as minimal plain classes with a `fromJson` constructor — one per scenario. Assert on schema shape (field counts, leaf types, branch presence, unresolved set) rather than on exception messages.
scenarios: Given a flat DTO with fields (name: String, age: int, isActive: bool), When probed, Then the schema reports three leaves with matching types and empty unresolved.
Given a DTO with a nested DTO field, When probed, Then the parent schema contains a branch whose inner schema has the nested fields.
Given a DTO that reads a list field `tags` as `List<dynamic>` via the generated code pattern, When probed, Then the schema contains a leaf of type List and the probe does not crash.
Given a DTO that reads a field via a converter expecting an unsupported type, When probed, Then the field lands in `unresolved` and the probe terminates within the attempt bound.
Given the same probe invocation run twice, When the schemas are compared, Then they are equal — proving determinism in widening order.
testing.refactor_for_testability: If the probe loop is hard to drive synchronously, isolate the widening logic into a private method that can be tested in isolation on a fake `fromJson` closure. Do not make anything public.

quality_bar.maintainability: Adding a new candidate type is one entry in the candidate list plus one corresponding stand-in factory.
quality_bar.dry: The widen-and-replay loop lives in one place.
quality_bar.single_responsibility: The map only knows about schema discovery — it does not own generator logic.
quality_bar.performance: Probing runs once per dummy api construction, bounded by candidateCount * maxFields; O(1) extra allocations per iteration.
quality_bar.abuse_resistance: Infinite loops are impossible because the attempt counter strictly bounds iterations.
